name: Decouple AltID from Apple/Google
consequences: Removing the single point of failure in the current MitID setup will reduce Denmark's digital sovereignty risk, but it may also increase complexity and costs for public services that currently rely on platform-controlled signals.
options: Implement a separate identity verification system using open standards like FIDO2/WebAuthn
Develop a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path to reduce dependence on mobile OS duopoly
Establish a national digital identity hub that aggregates and verifies user identities across multiple platforms
review: Decoupling AltID from Apple/Google will create trade-offs between complexity and cost, but the project should focus on establishing a platform-neutral fallback access path to reduce Denmark's digital sovereignty risk.

name: Influence EU Digital Identity Wallet framework
consequences: Shaping European requirements on interoperability, portability, resilience, and reduced platform lock-in will have a direct impact on Danish digital identity planning, but it may also require significant coordination with EU bodies and technical working groups.
options: Engage with relevant EU bodies to include platform-neutral access requirements in the Digital Identity Wallet framework
Develop a technical proposal for a European-level fallback authentication mechanism that can be adopted by multiple member states
Establish a coalition of Nordic countries to advocate for common digital identity standards and policies
review: Influencing the EU Digital Identity Wallet framework will introduce trade-offs between coordination and impact, but the project should focus on shaping European requirements that support platform-neutral access or certified fallback mechanisms.

name: Secure formal written response from Digitaliseringsstyrelsen
consequences: Obtaining a formal written response will demonstrate progress toward the project's objective, but it may also require significant engagement with relevant authorities and stakeholders.
options: Develop a policy proposal that addresses the feasibility and risk report, as well as the fallback-authentication concept note
Establish a working group with Digitaliseringsstyrelsen to discuss and refine the proposal
Organize a public consultation to gather feedback from relevant stakeholders
review: Securing a formal written response will create trade-offs between engagement and progress, but the project should focus on demonstrating progress toward its objective.

name: Build credibility through demonstrator work
consequences: Producing credible technical evidence will increase the project's influence on digital identity planning, but it may also require significant investment in non-production demonstrators.
options: Develop a mobile-oriented demonstrator that authenticates against a simulated Danish public-sector identity-provider environment using open standards like FIDO2/WebAuthn
Explore a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path to reduce dependence on the mobile OS duopoly
Invest in a hybrid approach that combines elements of both demonstrators
review: Building credibility through demonstrator work will introduce trade-offs between investment and influence, but the project should focus on producing credible technical evidence that supports platform-neutral access or certified fallback mechanisms.

name: Establish a coalition of stakeholders
consequences: Building a coalition will increase the project's influence and credibility, but it may also require significant coordination and engagement with relevant stakeholders.
options: Engage with civil-society organisations, privacy advocates, academic researchers, security experts, contingency-planning stakeholders, and sympathetic members of Folketinget's digital policy committee
Develop a coalition-building strategy that focuses on key influencers and decision-makers
Organize regular stakeholder meetings to discuss progress and gather feedback
review: Establishing a coalition will create trade-offs between coordination and influence, but the project should focus on building a broad coalition of stakeholders.

name: Secure funding for phase two
consequences: Obtaining funding for phase two will allow the project to proceed with demonstrator work, but it may also require significant engagement with funders and stakeholders.
options: Develop a detailed financing plan that covers the remainder of the project
Engage with potential funders to discuss and refine the proposal
Organize a public consultation to gather feedback from relevant stakeholders
review: Securing funding for phase two will introduce trade-offs between engagement and progress, but the project should focus on demonstrating progress toward its objective.

name: Influence procurement processes around AltID
consequences: Shaping procurement requirements will have a direct impact on Danish digital identity planning, but it may also require significant coordination with relevant authorities and stakeholders.
options: Develop a policy proposal that addresses the feasibility and risk report, as well as the fallback-authentication concept note
Establish a working group with Digitaliseringsstyrelsen to discuss and refine the proposal
Organize a public consultation to gather feedback from relevant stakeholders
review: Influencing procurement processes will create trade-offs between coordination and impact, but the project should focus on shaping requirements that support platform-neutral access or certified fallback mechanisms.

name: Build a strong technical team
consequences: Assembling a strong technical team will increase the project's credibility and influence, but it may also require significant investment in staffing and training.
options: Hire a lead researcher in mobile security and authentication protocols
Recruit a regulatory and compliance specialist familiar with Danish and EU digital identity frameworks
Invest in a technical lead for demonstrator work and additional engineers or contractors
review: Building a strong technical team will introduce trade-offs between investment and credibility, but the project should focus on assembling a diverse and experienced team.

name: Engage the Danish Parliament's IT Committee
consequences: Securing a formal hearing before the committee can validate the project's platform-neutrality and fallback-access goals, but may also attract scrutiny from Apple/Google lobbyists.
options: Testify with technical evidence on the feasibility of platform-neutral digital identity
Lobby for explicit language in parliamentary reports highlighting supplier-concentration risks
Collaborate with committee members to draft legislation requiring platform-neutrality in Danish public services
review: Engaging the Danish Parliament's IT Committee introduces a trade-off between securing formal validation and facing scrutiny from Apple/Google lobbyists. This lever leaves unaddressed the potential for coalition-building efforts to be overshadowed by more vocal or well-funded interests.

name: Develop a Proof-of-Concept (PoC) on FIDO2/WebAuthn
consequences: A successful PoC can demonstrate the feasibility of platform-neutral authentication, but may also inadvertently create unrealistic expectations about deployability or scalability.
options: Implement a minimal viable product (MVP) that authenticates against a simulated Danish public-sector identity-provider environment
Explore the use of FIDO2/WebAuthn with a focus on device-agnostic and platform-neutral authentication flows
Develop a PoC that integrates FIDO2/WebAuthn with existing MitID infrastructure, highlighting potential for incremental upgrades
review: Developing a Proof-of-Concept on FIDO2/WebAuthn introduces a trade-off between demonstrating feasibility and creating unrealistic expectations about deployability or scalability. This lever leaves unaddressed the need to prioritize coalition-building efforts over technical innovation.

name: Build a Stronger Coalition with Civil Society
consequences: A more robust coalition can amplify the project's voice and influence, but may also create tensions or conflicts within the group.
options: Engage with civil society organizations to co-create awareness campaigns highlighting supplier-concentration risks
Collaborate with advocacy groups to draft policy briefs that emphasize platform-neutrality and fallback-access requirements
Host workshops and roundtables to build consensus among coalition members on key goals and messaging
review: Building a stronger coalition with civil society introduces a trade-off between amplifying the project's voice and influence, and creating tensions or conflicts within the group. This lever leaves unaddressed the need to prioritize technical innovation over coalition-building efforts.

name: Develop a Fallback-Access Demonstration
consequences: A successful demonstration can showcase the feasibility of fallback-access, but may also highlight the complexity and challenges associated with implementing such a system.
options: Implement a minimal viable product (MVP) that authenticates against a simulated Danish public-sector identity-provider environment using FIDO2/WebAuthn
Explore the use of hardware tokens or browser-based fallback-access methods, highlighting potential for incremental upgrades to MitID infrastructure
Develop a demonstration that integrates fallback-access with existing MitID infrastructure, emphasizing potential for incremental upgrades
review: Developing a fallback-access demonstration introduces a trade-off between showcasing feasibility and highlighting complexity and challenges. This lever leaves unaddressed the need to prioritize coalition-building efforts over technical innovation.

name: Engage with the European Digital Identity Association
consequences: Securing membership or influence within the association can provide a platform for amplifying the project's voice and goals, but may also require compromise on core objectives.
options: Join the association as an individual member to contribute to discussions on digital identity standards and best practices
Collaborate with the association to develop guidelines and recommendations for platform-neutral digital identity
Lobby for inclusion of fallback-access requirements in the association's policy documents
review: Engaging with the European Digital Identity Association introduces a trade-off between amplifying the project's voice and goals, and requiring compromise on core objectives. This lever leaves unaddressed the need to prioritize coalition-building efforts over EU-level engagement.

name: Reframe Danish Public Sector Identity Policy
consequences: This lever directly impacts how the Danish public sector approaches digital identity, shifting focus from convenience to resilience and supplier concentration risk.
options: Embed platform-neutrality in policy frameworks
Incorporate continuity of service into national eIDAS2 requirements
Develop a formalized procurement process for fallback authentication protocols
review: This lever introduces the primary trade-off between convenience and resilience, but leaves unaddressed the gap in technical standards and certification processes that need to be adapted for platform-neutral access.

name: Develop a Fallback Authentication Standard
consequences: This lever creates a new standard for fallback authentication, which can reduce dependence on Apple/Google but introduces the risk of vendor lock-in with the chosen standard.
options: Adopt an open standards approach using FIDO2/WebAuthn and OpenID Connect
Develop a proprietary standard for fallback authentication
Collaborate with EU bodies to create a harmonized European digital identity wallet standard
review: This lever introduces the trade-off between vendor lock-in and standardization, but leaves unaddressed the gap in how this new standard will be adopted by existing operators.

name: Influence AltID Procurement Processes
consequences: This lever directly impacts the procurement processes for AltID, ensuring that platform-neutral and fallback-capable solutions are considered, but may introduce delays due to institutional inertia.
options: Embed platform-neutrality in AltID tender requirements
Develop a separate RFP process for fallback authentication protocols
Collaborate with EU bodies to create harmonized procurement standards
review: This lever introduces the trade-off between institutional inertia and procurement influence, but leaves unaddressed the gap in how this new approach will be received by existing operators.

name: Establish a Fallback Authentication Demonstrator
consequences: This lever creates a working demonstrator for fallback authentication, which can provide credible technical evidence but may not directly influence procurement processes.
options: Develop a mobile-oriented demonstrator using open standards
Create a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path
Collaborate with EU bodies to develop a harmonized European digital identity wallet demonstrator
review: This lever introduces the trade-off between technical credibility and procurement influence, but leaves unaddressed the gap in how this new approach will be integrated into existing systems.

name: Engage with EU Digital Identity Associations
consequences: This lever directly impacts EU-level engagement, ensuring that Danish advocacy aligns with broader European requirements on interoperability and reduced platform lock-in.
options: Collaborate with the European Digital Identity Association to create harmonized standards
Develop a joint position paper on platform-neutral access with other EU member states
Engage in technical working groups connected to the European Digital Identity Wallet framework
review: This lever introduces the trade-off between EU-level engagement and domestic influence, but leaves unaddressed the gap in how this new approach will be integrated into existing national policies.

name: Build a Coalition of Stakeholders
consequences: This lever creates a coalition of stakeholders that can support platform-neutral and fallback-capable solutions, but may introduce conflicting interests and priorities.
options: Collaborate with civil society organizations to promote digital sovereignty
Develop a joint position paper on platform-neutrality with industry experts
Engage in advocacy work with sympathetic members of the Folketinget's digital policy committee
review: This lever introduces the trade-off between coalition-building and conflicting interests, but leaves unaddressed the gap in how this new approach will be integrated into existing decision-making processes.
